title: The Layers of AI — From Chatbots to Agents

scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: The layers of AI. [pause] A mental map — from simple chatbots to autonomous agent systems.

scenes.name: Why layers
scenes.text: Most teachers meet AI through a chatbot window. [pause] But what you see there is one layer. There are six. [pause] Each one extends the capabilities of the one before it.

scenes.name: Stack
scenes.text: From the ground up. [pause] Layer 1 — text chatbots. Layer 2 — multimodal. Layer 3 — tool use. Layer 4 — computer use. Layer 5 — agents. Layer 6 — agent systems.

scenes.name: L1 to L2
scenes.text: Layer 1 is what most teachers use today — text in, text out. [pause] Layer 2 adds eyes and ears — images, audio, video. [pause] There is enormous value at these two layers alone.

scenes.name: L3
scenes.text: Layer 3 is when models start to <emphasis>use tools</emphasis>. [pause] They search the web. Analyze a spreadsheet. Run calculations. [pause] AI moves from knowing things — to finding things out.

scenes.name: L4 to L5
scenes.text: Layer 4 lets AI operate your computer — open apps, click buttons, fill forms. [pause] Layer 5 is the shift to agents. [pause] You give a goal. The agent plans the steps.

scenes.name: L6
scenes.text: Layer 6 — agent systems. [pause] A team of agents working together. One researches, one designs, one creates, one checks. [pause] Early days, but the direction is clear.

scenes.name: Where you
scenes.text: Most teachers today work at layers one and two. [pause] That is fine. [pause] The point of the map is not to climb it — it is to know what each layer offers, and where the tools are heading.

scenes.name: Closing
scenes.text: More capable AI. [pause] More important teacher.
